chat.confirm_clear_msg: Start a new session? Short-term memory will be cleared.
chat.error_timeout: ⚠️ Timeout: The agent took too long. Please try again.
chat.file_attached_instructions: You can find the file at this path. Process or analyze it as needed.
chat.greeting: Hello! I am AuraGo. What task can I do for you today?
chat.session_cleared_fallback: Session cleared. How can I help?
chat.sse_error_recovery: Script had an error. Fixing code...
dashboard.prompt_no_data: No data yet. Statistics appear after the first conversation and are cleared on server restart.
help.agent.additional_prompt: Arbitrary text that is always appended at the end of the system prompt. Ideal for user-specific rules, context info, or fixed instructions that should apply to every request.
help.agent.allow_filesystem_write: When disabled, only read operations (list, read) are allowed. Write operations (write, delete, move, mkdir…) are blocked.
help.agent.allow_mcp: ⚠️ Danger Zone: Allows the agent to connect to external MCP servers (Model Context Protocol) and use their tools. MCP servers are external processes that can execute arbitrary code — only add trusted servers!
help.agent.allow_network_requests: When disabled, the agent blocks all api_request calls. Other integrations (Telegram, Discord…) are unaffected.
help.agent.allow_python: When disabled, the agent blocks execute_python, execute_skill and save_tool.
help.agent.allow_remote_shell: When disabled, execute_remote_shell / remote_execution are blocked.
help.agent.allow_self_update: When disabled, the agent cannot run manage_updates (no automatic self-updates).
help.agent.allow_shell: When disabled, the agent blocks all execute_shell requests with a PERMISSION DENIED error.
help.agent.allow_web_scraper: Deprecated — use tools.web_scraper.enabled in the 'Tools & Services' section instead.
help.agent.context_window: Context window size in tokens. 0 = auto-detect.
help.agent.core_memory_cap_mode: Behavior when the cap is reached: 'soft' adds the entry and returns a warning, 'hard' rejects new entries. Default: soft.
help.agent.core_memory_max_entries: Maximum number of core memory entries. 0 = unlimited. Default: 200.
help.agent.debug_mode: Enables debug mode with extended error messages.
help.agent.memory_compression_char_limit: Character limit after which old messages are compressed.
help.agent.personality_engine: Enables the personality engine with mood and micro-traits. Automatically enabled when Personality Engine V2 is active.
help.agent.personality_engine_v2: Enables the advanced V2 engine with asynchronous LLM mood analysis. Automatically enables V1 as well (required dependency).
help.agent.personality_v2_api_key: Optional custom API key for the V2 engine. Usually 'ollama' for Ollama.
help.agent.personality_v2_model: Model name for the Personality Engine V2, e.g. 'gpt-4o-mini' or 'llama3.1'.
help.agent.personality_v2_provider: Select a provider from the provider management for V2 personality analysis. Empty = main LLM.
help.agent.personality_v2_timeout_secs: Timeout in seconds for the asynchronous V2 mood analysis LLM call. Default: 30.
help.agent.personality_v2_url: Optional custom base URL for V2, e.g. for a local Ollama ('http://localhost:11434/v1').
help.agent.show_tool_results: Shows tool results in the chat window as collapsible debug blocks.
help.agent.step_delay_seconds: Delay between tool calls in seconds. 0 = no delay.
help.agent.sudo_enabled: Enables execute_sudo. The sudo password must be stored in the vault under the key 'sudo_password'.
help.agent.system_prompt_token_budget: Maximum token count for the system prompt. 0 = unlimited.
help.agent.user_profiling: Enables automatic user profiling: the agent detects preferences and habits from the chat and stores them (requires Personality Engine V2).
help.agent.user_profiling_threshold: Minimum confidence (number of confirmations) before a profile attribute is injected into the system prompt. Default: 2.
help.agent.workflow_feedback: Enables occasional status updates during long workflows (e.g. 'One moment please...').
help.ansible.default_inventory: (Local mode only) Default inventory file (e.g. '/etc/ansible/hosts'). Env var: ANSIBLE_INVENTORY.
help.ansible.enabled: Enables the Ansible integration. Supports two modes: 'sidecar' (Docker API) and 'local' (ansible CLI directly on the host).
help.ansible.mode: Execution mode: 'sidecar' = Ansible API container (Docker), 'local' = call ansible binaries directly on the host. Default: 'sidecar'.
help.ansible.playbooks_dir: (Local mode only) Directory containing Ansible playbooks (e.g. '/opt/ansible/playbooks'). Env var: ANSIBLE_PLAYBOOKS_DIR.
help.ansible.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only check status, inventory, and list playbooks but cannot run ad-hoc commands or playbooks.
help.ansible.timeout: Maximum runtime for Ansible commands in seconds (default: 300). Increase for long-running playbooks.
help.ansible.token: (Sidecar mode only) Bearer token for the Ansible sidecar API. Must match ANSIBLE_API_TOKEN in the container.
help.ansible.url: (Sidecar mode only) URL of the Ansible API server. Default: 'http://ansible:5001'.
help.brave_search.api_key: Brave Search subscription token (X-Subscription-Token). Get a free or paid key at https://brave.com/search/api/ — the free tier allows 2000 requests/month.
help.brave_search.country: Default country code for localised results, e.g. 'DE' or 'US'. Leave blank for global search.
help.brave_search.enabled: Enable the Brave Search integration. Allows web searches via the Brave Search API with high-quality, up-to-date results.
help.brave_search.lang: Default search language, e.g. 'de' or 'en'. The agent can override this per query.
help.budget.default_cost.input_per_milli: Default cost for 1 million input tokens if model is not listed.
help.budget.default_cost.output_per_mill: Default cost for 1 million output tokens if model is not listed.
help.budget.enforcement: Action on budget exceed: 'warn' = warning only, 'partial' = limited tools, 'full' = stop.
help.budget.reset_hour: Hour of the day (0-23) when the daily budget resets.
help.budget.warning_threshold: Percentage (0.0 to 1.0) at which a warning is triggered.
help.chromecast.enabled: Enables Chromecast integration for audio output.
help.chromecast.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only discover Chromecast devices and check status but cannot play, speak, or control them.
help.chromecast.tts_port: Local port for the TTS file server to stream to Chromecast.
help.circuit_breaker.llm_timeout_seconds: Timeout for LLM requests in seconds.
help.circuit_breaker.maintenance_timeout: Time in minutes without user activity before the agent automatically returns from maintenance mode.
help.circuit_breaker.max_tool_calls: Maximum number of tool calls allowed per request.
help.circuit_breaker.retry_intervals: Wait times between retry attempts, e.g. '30s', '2m', '10m'.
help.co_agents.llm.provider: Select a provider from the provider management for Co-Agents. Empty = main LLM.
help.co_agents.max_concurrent: Maximum number of concurrently running Co-Agents.
help.discord.allowed_user_id: The only User ID the Discord bot will listen to.
help.discord.bot_token: Discord bot token from the Developer Portal.
help.discord.default_channel_id: The ID of the default channel for startup messages.
help.discord.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read Discord messages but cannot send them.
help.docker.host: Optional remote Docker host (e.g. tcp://192.168.1.5:2375). Leave blank for local socket.
help.docker.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list and inspect Docker containers but cannot start, stop, create, or remove them.
help.egg_mode.egg_id: Unique egg ID (assigned by master). Env: AURAGO_EGG_ID.
help.egg_mode.enabled: Enables egg mode: This instance is a worker egg controlled by a master. Env: AURAGO_EGG_MODE.
help.egg_mode.master_url: WebSocket URL of the master (e.g. ws://master:8080/api/invasion/ws). Env: AURAGO_MASTER_URL.
help.egg_mode.nest_id: Nest ID where this egg is deployed. Env: AURAGO_NEST_ID.
help.egg_mode.shared_key: Shared HMAC key for master authentication. Env: AURAGO_SHARED_KEY.
help.email.from_address: Sender email address to be transmitted.
help.email.imap_host: IMAP server address for email reception.
help.email.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read emails but cannot send them.
help.email.smtp_host: SMTP server address for email sending.
help.email.watch_enabled: Watches the email inbox for new messages.
help.email.watch_folder: The mailbox folder to check for new mails (usually INBOX).
help.email.watch_interval_seconds: Interval in seconds to check the mailbox.
help.email_accounts.from_address: Sender email address. Empty = same as username.
help.email_accounts.id: Unique identifier of the email account (e.g. personal, work).
help.email_accounts.imap_host: IMAP server address for email reception.
help.email_accounts.name: Display name of the email account.
help.email_accounts.smtp_host: SMTP server address for email sending.
help.email_accounts.watch_enabled: Enables the inbox watcher for this account.
help.email_accounts.watch_folder: The mailbox folder to check for new mails (usually INBOX).
help.email_accounts.watch_interval_secon: Interval in seconds to check this account's mailbox.
help.embeddings.provider: Provider from the provider management used for embeddings. Model and URL are taken from the provider entry. 'disabled' turns off long-term memory entirely.
help.fallback_llm.enabled: Enables automatic failover to a backup LLM on errors.
help.fallback_llm.error_threshold: Number of sequential errors before switching to fallback model.
help.fallback_llm.probe_interval_seconds: Interval in seconds to probe and try restoring the primary model.
help.fallback_llm.provider: Provider from provider management for the fallback LLM. Model, URL and API key are taken from the provider entry.
help.github.base_url: Base URL of the GitHub API. Default: 'https://api.github.com'. For GitHub Enterprise, enter your own API URL.
help.github.default_private: When enabled, new repositories are created as 'private' by default. Otherwise as 'public'.
help.github.enabled: Enables the GitHub integration for repository and issue management.
help.github.owner: GitHub username or organization used as the default owner for API calls.
help.github.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read repos, issues, and PRs but cannot create, delete, or modify files.
help.home_assistant.access_token: Long-lived access token for Home Assistant auth.
help.home_assistant.enabled: Enables Home Assistant smart home integration.
help.home_assistant.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read device states but cannot call services (no toggling devices).
help.home_assistant.url: URL of the Home Assistant instance, e.g. 'http://192.168.1.100:8123'.
help.indexing.directories: List of directories to watch and index. Relative paths are resolved from the config directory. Default: ./knowledge.
help.indexing.enabled: Enables automatic file indexing. Files in configured folders are periodically scanned, converted to embeddings, and available to the agent as a knowledge base.
help.indexing.extensions: List of file extensions to index. Text files are read directly, documents (.pdf, .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .odt, .rtf) are automatically extracted. Binary files (executables) are automatically rejected. Default: .txt, .md, .json, .csv, .log, .yaml, .yml, .pdf, .docx, .xlsx, .pptx, .odt, .rtf.
help.indexing.index_images: When enabled, image files (.jpg, .png, .gif, .webp, .bmp) are sent to the Vision LLM for analysis. The result is stored with filename and path in the embeddings. Warning: Can cause significant API costs with many images.
help.indexing.poll_interval_seconds: How often (in seconds) directories are checked for changes. Default: 60.
help.invasion_control.enabled: Enables the Invasion Control UI for managing sub-agents (Eggs) and deployment targets (Nests).
help.invasion_control.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list nests and eggs but cannot assign, hatch, stop, or send tasks/secrets.
help.koofr.app_password: App password for the Koofr API.
help.koofr.base_url: Base URL of the Koofr server (usually https://app.koofr.net).
help.koofr.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read and list files but cannot write, delete, or move them.
help.koofr.username: Email address for the Koofr login.
help.llm.provider: Provider from provider management for the main LLM. Model, URL and API key are taken from the provider entry.
help.llm.structured_outputs: ⚠️ Enables Structured Outputs (strict mode) for tool calls. ONLY enable for models that support this (e.g. GPT-4o, GPT-4o-mini). Incompatible models will return errors!
help.llm.temperature: Sampling temperature for the language model (0.1–1.5). Lower values → more deterministic, higher → more creative. Default: 0.7. The personality engine slightly adjusts this value automatically (±0.1).
help.llm.use_native_functions: ⚠️ Uses native function calling of the LLM instead of JSON-based tool dispatch. Only enable if the model supports this (e.g. GPT-4, Claude, Gemini). Free-tier models often do NOT support this!
help.logging.enable_file_log: Writes logs additionally to a file.
help.maintenance.enabled: Enables the daily maintenance mode for self-updates.
help.maintenance.time: Time for the daily maintenance run (HH:MM format).
help.mcp.enabled: Enables the MCP integration. Additionally requires 'MCP Server Connections' to be enabled in the Danger Zone.
help.mcp.servers: List of external MCP servers that AuraGo should connect to. Each server is launched as a child process via stdio (JSON-RPC 2.0).
help.meshcentral.blocked_operations: Explicit deny-list for MeshCentral operations (e.g. ["run_command","power_action","wake"]). This policy is enforced in addition to readonly.
help.meshcentral.enabled: Enables the MeshCentral integration for remote device management.
help.meshcentral.login_token: Login token username (starts with ~t:), generated via MeshCentral → My Account → Login Tokens. Bypasses 2FA. The token password goes into the password field above.
help.meshcentral.password: Password for login. When using a login token: the token password generated by MeshCentral.
help.meshcentral.readonly: Read-only mode for MeshCentral. Allows only read operations (list groups/devices) and blocks changes like wake, power actions, or command execution.
help.meshcentral.url: Root URL of the MeshCentral installation (e.g. https://mesh.example.com or https://host/meshcentral). If MeshCentral runs on a sub-path, include it. No trailing slash.
help.meshcentral.username: Regular username for MeshCentral login. Ignored when a login token is set.
help.mqtt.broker: MQTT broker address in the format 'tcp://host:port' (e.g. 'tcp://localhost:1883') or 'ssl://host:port' for TLS.
help.mqtt.client_id: Client ID for the broker connection. Default: 'aurago'. Must be unique per broker.
help.mqtt.enabled: Enables the MQTT integration. Allows the agent to publish, receive, and subscribe to MQTT messages.
help.mqtt.qos: Default Quality of Service level: 0 = at most once, 1 = at least once, 2 = exactly once.
help.mqtt.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read MQTT messages but cannot publish, subscribe, or unsubscribe.
help.mqtt.relay_to_agent: When enabled, incoming MQTT messages are relayed as context to the agent.
help.mqtt.topics: Comma-separated list of topics to subscribe to on startup (e.g. 'home/#, sensors/temperature').
help.mqtt.username: Username for MQTT authentication (optional, depending on broker configuration).
help.notifications.ntfy.topic: ntfy topic name for this AuraGo instance.
help.notifications.ntfy.url: ntfy server URL. Default: 'https://ntfy.sh'. Can be self-hosted.
help.ollama.enabled: Enables the Ollama integration for local LLM management.
help.ollama.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list and show models but cannot pull, delete, load, or unload them.
help.ollama.url: URL of the Ollama API. Default: 'http://localhost:11434'.
help.proxmox.enabled: Enables the Proxmox VE integration for virtualization management.
help.proxmox.insecure: Disable TLS certificate verification. Enable for self-signed certificates.
help.proxmox.node: Default node name, e.g. 'pve'. Used when no node is specified.
help.proxmox.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list and inspect VMs/containers but cannot start, stop, or create snapshots.
help.proxmox.token_id: API token ID in the format 'user@pam!tokenname'.
help.proxmox.url: URL of the Proxmox VE API, e.g. 'https://pve.example.com:8006'.
help.rocketchat.alias: Display name of the bot in Rocket.Chat. Default: 'AuraGo'.
help.rocketchat.channel: Default channel to listen on, e.g. 'general'.
help.rocketchat.url: URL of the Rocket.Chat instance, e.g. 'https://chat.example.com'.
help.rocketchat.user_id: Bot user ID from Rocket.Chat (Profile > Personal Access Tokens).
help.sandbox.auto_install: Automatically install the llm-sandbox Python package (with MCP extras) on startup. Installs 'llm-sandbox[mcp-docker]' or 'llm-sandbox[mcp-podman]' depending on the backend. Requires a working Python/pip environment.
help.sandbox.backend: Container backend: 'docker' (default) or 'podman'. Docker must be installed and reachable.
help.sandbox.docker_host: Docker host address (e.g. 'unix:///var/run/docker.sock' or 'tcp://localhost:2375'). Empty = auto-detect or inherit from docker.host.
help.sandbox.enabled: Enables isolated code execution in Docker containers. The agent will use execute_sandbox as the default tool for writing and running code.
help.sandbox.keep_alive: Keeps the sandbox MCP server running between calls instead of restarting it each time.
help.sandbox.network_enabled: Allows sandbox containers to access the network. Disabled by default for maximum isolation.
help.sandbox.pool_size: Number of pre-warmed containers in the pool (0 = no pooling). Higher values speed up execution but use more resources.
help.sandbox.timeout_seconds: Maximum execution time per sandbox call in seconds (default: 30).
help.server.bridge_address: TCP address for the Lifeboat bridge connection (host:port). Default: 'localhost:8089'.
help.server.host: IP address the server listens on. '127.0.0.1' = local only, '0.0.0.0' = all interfaces.
help.server.max_body_bytes: Maximum HTTP request body size in bytes (config endpoint). Default: 10485760 (10 MB).
help.server.port: Port for the web server and REST API.
help.sqlite.invasion_path: Path to SQLite database for Invasion Control (Nests/Eggs). Default: './data/invasion.db'.
help.tailscale.api_key: Tailscale API key (starts with 'tskey-api-'). Create at https://login.tailscale.com/admin/settings/keys.
help.tailscale.enabled: Enables the Tailscale integration for VPN network management.
help.tailscale.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list devices and status but cannot enable or disable routes.
help.tailscale.tailnet: Tailnet name, e.g. 'example.com', or leave blank for the default tailnet ('-').
help.telegram.max_concurrent_workers: Maximum number of concurrently processed Telegram messages. Default: 5.
help.telegram.telegram_user_id: Your Telegram user ID for authorization.
help.tools.inventory.enabled: Enables device inventory queries. Allows the agent to query registered devices.
help.tools.knowledge_graph.enabled: Enables the knowledge graph tool. Allows the agent to manage nodes and edges in the graph.
help.tools.knowledge_graph.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only search the graph but cannot add or delete nodes or edges.
help.tools.memory.enabled: Enables core memory tools (manage_memory, query_memory). Allows the agent to store and retrieve facts.
help.tools.memory.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read memories but cannot add, update, or delete them.
help.tools.memory_maintenance.enabled: Enables memory maintenance tools (archive_memory, optimize_memory). Allows the agent to archive and optimize memories.
help.tools.missions.enabled: Enables Mission Control (manage_missions). Allows the agent to manage automation missions.
help.tools.missions.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only view missions but cannot create, update, delete, or run them.
help.tools.notes.enabled: Enables notes/todo management (manage_notes). Allows the agent to manage notes and tasks.
help.tools.notes.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only view notes but cannot create, update, or delete them.
help.tools.scheduler.enabled: Enables the scheduler tool (cron_scheduler). Allows the agent to schedule recurring tasks.
help.tools.scheduler.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only view scheduled jobs but cannot add or remove them.
help.tools.secrets_vault.enabled: Enables the secrets vault tool. Allows the agent to read and store encrypted secrets.
help.tools.secrets_vault.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read secrets but cannot store or modify them.
help.tools.stop_process.enabled: Enables the ability to stop processes. When disabled, the agent can only list processes.
help.tools.web_scraper.enabled: When disabled, the web_scraper skill is blocked. The agent will not be able to fetch external websites.
help.tools.web_scraper.summary_mode: Scraped content is sent to a separate (cheap) model that creates a summary only. The agent receives only the summary — saves tokens and prevents prompt injection.
help.tools.web_scraper.summary_provider: Provider entry for the summarisation model. Empty = main LLM is used. A cheap model is recommended here.
help.tools.wol.enabled: Enables the Wake-on-LAN tool. The agent can send a magic packet to wake up devices that have a MAC address registered in the device manager. Requires devices to support WOL and be reachable on the local network.
help.tts.elevenlabs.voice_id: ElevenLabs Voice ID of the speaker.
help.tts.language: BCP-47 language code for Google TTS, e.g. 'de' or 'en'.
help.virustotal.api_key: API Key for VirusTotal tool. WARNING: DO NOT use this tool on files containing personal data or sensitive information. Files may be made available to security researchers and your submissions may be made public.
help.virustotal.enabled: Enable the VirusTotal integration. Allows scanning URLs, domains, IPs, and file hashes via the VirusTotal API.
help.vision.api_key: API Key for the vision LLM.
help.vision.base_url: Base URL for the vision LLM.
help.vision.model: Model name for the vision LLM.
help.vision.provider: Select a provider from the provider management for image analysis.
help.web_config.enabled: Enables the web configuration page at /config. Disabled by default for security.
help.webdav.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only read and list files but cannot write, delete, or move them.
help.webhooks.enabled: Enables the webhook system. Allows up to 10 incoming webhooks with token authentication.
help.webhooks.max_payload_size: Maximum payload size in bytes per webhook request. Default: 65536 (64 KB).
help.webhooks.rate_limit: Maximum webhook requests per minute per token. 0 = unlimited.
help.webhooks.read_only: When enabled, the agent can only list webhooks and read logs but cannot create, update, or delete them.
help.whisper.api_key: API Key for the external Whisper provider.
help.whisper.base_url: Base URL for the Whisper provider.
help.whisper.mode: Whisper mode: 'whisper' (standard OpenAI API), 'multimodal' (Gemini/multimodal LLM) or 'local' (local server).
help.whisper.provider: Select a provider from the provider management for speech-to-text.
invasion.apikey_set: API Key is already set. Leave empty to keep it.
invasion.name: Name
invasion.secret_set: Secret is already set. Leave empty to keep it.
invasion.subtitle: Invasion Control
invasion.title: Invasion Control
invasion.validate_first: Please save first, then test connection.
invasion.will_delete_egg: will be permanently deleted. The associated API key will be removed from the vault.
invasion.will_delete_nest: will be permanently deleted. The associated secret will be removed from the vault.
login.subtitle: Please sign in to continue
mission.del_confirm: Are you sure you want to delete this mission?
mission.empty_desc: Create your first mission to automate tasks. Missions can run on demand or on a schedule.
mission.hint_cron_fmt: Format: sec min hour day month weekday
mission.hint_prompt: This is the message sent to the agent when the mission runs.
mission.placeholder_prompt: Describe what the agent should do...
mission.toast_cron_req: ❌ Cron expression required for scheduled missions
missions.empty_create_first: Create your first mission to get started
missions.empty_no_missions_of_type: No missions of this type available
missions.form_cron_hint: Format: Minute Hour Day Month Weekday
missions.form_prompt_placeholder: Describe the task for the agent...
missions.heading: 🎯 Mission Control
missions.trigger_egg_hatched_hint: Triggered when an egg has been successfully installed on a nest.
missions.trigger_mqtt_hint: Triggered when an MQTT message arrives on the configured topic.
missions.trigger_mqtt_payload_hint: Only trigger if the message content contains this text
missions.trigger_nest_cleared_hint: Triggered when a temporary nest is removed.
missions.trigger_webhook_hint: The webhook must be created in the Webhook Manager
setup.confirm_skip_setup: Skip setup? You can configure AuraGo later via /config.
setup.step0_base_url_hint: Automatically set based on the provider.
setup.step0_description: AuraGo requires at least one LLM connection to function. Choose your provider and enter the API key.
setup.step0_native_functions_desc: Uses the provider's native tool calls. Warning: Only enable if the model supports this (GPT-4, Claude, Gemini). Free-tier models often do NOT support this!
setup.step0_provider_hint: OpenRouter recommended — offers many models including free options.
setup.step1_description: These settings significantly improve AuraGo's capabilities. You can configure them now or later in the Config UI.
setup.step1_embeddings_apikey_hint: Only required when using an external provider.
setup.step1_embeddings_apikey_placeholde: Same key as LLM if same provider
setup.step2_core_personality_hint: Base personality — affects tone and communication style. Custom personalities can be created as .md files.
setup.step2_description: Fine-tune AuraGo's personality and advanced features. Everything can be changed later in the config interface.
setup.step2_personality_v2_desc: Dynamic mood and emotional reactions based on conversation context.
setup.step2_v2_apikey_hint: Optional — if empty, the main API key is used.
setup.step2_v2_apikey_placeholder: Empty = same key as main LLM
setup.step2_v2_model_hint: Small, fast model for mood analysis.
setup.step2_v2_url_placeholder: Empty = same URL as main LLM
setup.success_description:
AuraGo is now configured and ready.
The settings have been saved.
setup.success_restart_notice: ⚠️ A restart is recommended for all changes to take effect.
config.restart.confirm: Really restart AuraGo service? (Takes about 3-5 seconds)
config.restart.reloading: The page will reload automatically shortly...
config.field.json_array_hint: JSON array (objects with name, input_per_million, output_per_million)
config.section.server.label: Server
config.section.directories.label: Directories
config.section.directories.desc: File paths for data and prompts
config.section.sqlite.label: Databases
config.section.logging.label: Logging
config.section.maintenance.label: Maintenance
config.section.indexing.label: Indexing
config.section.web_config.label: Web Config & Login
config.section.secrets.label: Secrets
config.section.secrets.desc: Manage encrypted secrets in the vault
config.section.backup_restore.label: Backup & Restore
config.section.backup_restore.desc: Back up and restore the instance (.ago archive)
config.section.updates.label: Updates
config.section.updates.desc: Update AuraGo to the latest version
config.section.devices.label: Devices
config.section.devices.desc: Manage device registry (view, add, edit, delete)
config.section.providers.label: Providers
config.section.providers.desc: Central management of all LLM providers and models
config.section.agent.label: Agent
config.section.llm.label: LLM Settings
config.section.fallback_llm.label: Fallback LLM
config.section.embeddings.label: Embeddings
config.section.circuit_breaker.label: Circuit Breaker
config.section.budget.label: Budget
config.section.co_agents.label: Co-Agents
config.section.tools.label: Tool Permissions
config.section.tools.desc: Enable/disable built-in agent tools and set read-only mode

config.llm.info_banner: Model, URL and API key are taken from the provider entry in provider management. Only advanced LLM settings are configured here.
config.embeddings.info_banner: Embeddings convert text into numerical vectors, allowing the agent to find semantically similar content. This powers the agent's long-term memory: conversation content, notes and indexed files are stored as embeddings and retrieved automatically when relevant. Model and URL are taken from the selected provider entry.
config.budget.info_banner: Values that cannot be determined directly (e.g. local models without token reporting) are estimated internally. The displayed usage may differ from the actual usage. This feature does not replace setting spending limits directly at the provider or API.
config.tools.warning_banner: These settings should normally not be changed. Disabling tools will significantly limit the agent's capabilities.
config.master_key.label: Master Key
config.master_key.vault_exists: Vault exists – Master Key locked. Delete vault to set a new key.
config.master_key.no_vault: No vault found. Enter a new key, save, and restart AuraGo.
config.meshcentral.test_desc: Tests login and WebSocket handshake using the values entered above. Unchanged fields use the saved config values.
config.openrouter.model_is_free: This model is free to use
config.openrouter.costs_auto_imported: Costs will be automatically imported for budget management.
config.budget.model_cost_updated: 💰 Model costs for "{model}" updated in budget management.
config.providers.id_help: Unique identifier (e.g. main, vision-ollama). Lowercase, hyphens allowed.
config.providers.type_help: Provider type determines API compatibility and auto-fills the Base URL.
config.providers.keep_existing_key: Leave empty to keep the existing key.
config.providers.oauth_auth_url_help: OAuth2 authorization endpoint of the provider.
config.providers.keep_existing_secret: Leave empty to keep existing secret.
config.providers.url_empty_error: Base URL must not be empty.
config.providers.oauth_required_error: OAuth2: Auth URL, Token URL and Client ID are required.
config.providers.id_exists: A provider with this ID already exists.
config.providers.hint.ollama: API Key is optional for Ollama.
config.providers.hint.google: API Key from Google AI Studio.
config.email.empty: No email accounts configured. Add one!
config.email.id_help: Unique identifier (e.g. personal, work). Lowercase, hyphens allowed.
config.email.keep_password: Leave empty to keep the existing password.
config.email.id_exists: An account with this ID already exists.
config.email.host_required: Provide at least an IMAP or SMTP host.
config.secrets.no_vault_desc: Set a Master Key under Web Config → Server and restart AuraGo.
config.secrets.key_help: Unique name for the secret (e.g. github_token, sudo_password).
config.secrets.value_help: The secret value. Stored with AES-256 encryption.
config.secrets.delete_confirm: Really delete secret "{key}"? This action cannot be undone.
config.updates.warning_banner: The update replaces binaries, prompts and UI files. Your configuration and data are preserved. AuraGo will restart after the update.
config.updates.confirm_install:
The update will start now. AuraGo will restart automatically afterwards.

Please reload the page after about 30–60 seconds.
config.updates.running: Update running — please wait and reload the page…
config.updates.started: ✅ Update started — AuraGo is restarting. Reload in 60s…
config.backup.create_desc: Creates an .ago archive (ZIP) with config, prompts, data and skills. The archive can optionally be encrypted and imported on another instance.
config.backup.password_hint: Archive will be encrypted with AES-256-GCM. Leave empty for plain ZIP.
config.backup.import_warning: Important: Import overwrites existing files. Config, prompts and data will be replaced. A restart is recommended after import.
config.backup.password_required: Encrypted backup — please enter the password.
config.devices.delete_confirm: Really delete device "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
config.indexing.enabled_desc: Enables automatic file indexing. Files in configured folders are converted to embeddings and available to the agent as knowledge.
config.indexing.images: Images (analyzed via Vision LLM, when enabled)
config.indexing.poll_help: How often to check for changed files (default: 60s).
config.indexing.index_images_desc: When enabled, image files (.jpg, .png, .gif, .webp, .bmp) are sent to the Vision LLM. The image analysis is stored with filename and path in the embeddings.
config.indexing.image_notice_desc: Image analysis via Vision LLM can cause significant API costs when indexing many images. Each image is sent individually to the LLM and generates token costs. Recommendation: Only index folders with relevant images.
config.prompts.additional_prompt_label: Additional instructions (always appended at the end of the system prompt)
config.prompts.volatility_desc: How strongly external events shift mood. 0 = barely reactive, 2 = maximum swing.
config.prompts.empathy_desc: Amplifies empathy-related mood shifts. 0 = no empathy, 2 = highly empathetic.
config.prompts.loneliness_desc: How sensitive the persona is to lack of social interaction. 0 = immune, 2 = very sensitive.
config.prompts.decay_desc: How quickly personality traits and mood fade without new input. 0.1 = slow decay, 2 = very fast.
config.prompts.conflict_desc: How the persona responds to conflict or contradiction.
config.prompts.metadata_hint: Metadata is prepended automatically when saving.
config.prompts.core_readonly: Built-in persona – read-only. Create a copy to customise.
config.mcp.locked_notice: MCP is locked in the Danger Zone
config.mcp.locked_desc: Enable "MCP Server Connections" in the Danger Zone first before configuring MCP servers.
config.mcp.disabled_desc: Enable the toggle above to connect MCP servers.
config.mcp.name_exists: A server with this name already exists.
config.sandbox.disabled_desc: Enable the toggle above to use isolated code execution in Docker containers. Docker must be available.
config.web_scraper.disabled_desc: Enable the toggle above to allow the agent to fetch external web pages and analyse their content.
config.web_scraper.summary_desc:
Scraped web pages are sent to a separate LLM that creates a summary. The agent only receives the summary instead of the full page content.
Benefits: Saves tokens in the main model and prevents prompt injection from external content. A cheap model can be selected here.
config.danger.warning_desc: Here you decide how much power to grant your agent. Keep in mind that the agent can make mistakes — only enable what you truly need!
config.danger.sudo.title: Sudo Execution (execute_sudo)
config.danger.sudo.desc: Allows the agent to run commands with sudo privileges. Sudo password must be stored in the vault.
config.danger.shell.title: Shell Execution (execute_shell)
config.danger.shell.desc: Allows the agent to run arbitrary shell commands on the host system.
config.danger.python.title: Python / Skill Execution (execute_python / execute_skill / save_tool)
config.danger.python.desc: Allows the agent to execute arbitrary Python code and skills locally without sandbox and persist new tools. Code runs directly on the host system — use the sandbox for isolated execution instead.
config.danger.filesystem.title: Filesystem Write Access (filesystem write ops)
config.danger.filesystem.desc: Allows the agent to write, delete, move files and create directories via the filesystem tool.
config.danger.network.title: Network Requests (api_request)
config.danger.network.desc: Allows the agent to send arbitrary HTTP requests to external URLs.
config.danger.remote_shell.title: Remote Shell (execute_remote_shell)
config.danger.remote_shell.desc: Allows the agent to execute shell commands on remote servers via SSH.
config.danger.self_update.title: Self-Update (manage_updates)
config.danger.self_update.desc: Allows the agent to update itself and restart the process.
config.danger.mcp.title: MCP Server Connections (mcp_call)
config.danger.mcp.desc: Allows the agent to connect to external MCP servers and use their tools. ⚠ Only add trusted servers!
config.webhooks.disabled_desc: Enable webhooks.enabled in the configuration under Webhook Settings below.
config.tokens.created_notice: Token created! Copy now – shown only once.
config.auth.enable_desc: When enabled, authentication is required for all web access. Set a password first!
config.auth.password_is_set: ✅ Password is set. Change it here:
config.auth.password_not_set: ⚠️ No password set — login guard cannot be activated.
config.auth.password_min_length: Password must be at least 8 characters.
config.auth.totp_desc: Additional security via authenticator apps (Google Authenticator, Authy, etc.).
config.auth.totp_instructions:
1. Scan the QR code with your authenticator app or enter the key manually.
2. Enter the 6-digit code to confirm.
config.vault.delete_desc:
This will permanently delete the vault. All stored API keys, passwords and credentials will be lost forever!

Type delete to continue:
config.vault.deleted_toast: ✓ Vault deleted. Enter a new master key, save, and restart AuraGo.
